verses.text: Be not many masters, knowing 'we shall receive the greater condemnation'; in many things we all offend. Teach humbly. Those who instruct are judged more strictly.
Be not many masters, knowing 'we shall receive the greater condemnation'; in many things we all offend. Teach humbly. Those who instruct are judged more strictly.
As bit and rudder turn horse and ship, so 'the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things'; how great a fire it kindles. Small but mighty. The little tongue steers the whole life.
As bit and rudder turn horse and ship, so 'the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things'; how great a fire it kindles. Small but mighty. The little tongue steers the whole life.
As bit and rudder turn horse and ship, so 'the tongue is a little member, and boasteth great things'; how great a fire it kindles. Small but mighty. The little tongue steers the whole life.
The tongue is 'a fire, a world of iniquity,' set on fire of hell; 'no man can tame the tongue,' an unruly evil. Untamed. The tongue's poison is beyond human mastery.

The tongue is 'a fire, a world of iniquity,' set on fire of hell; 'no man can tame the tongue,' an unruly evil. Untamed. The tongue's poison is beyond human mastery.
With it we bless God and curse men; 'these things ought not so to be.' Can a fountain yield sweet and bitter? Two streams. One mouth should not pour blessing and cursing.

Who is wise? Let him show it by good conduct; but bitter envying and strife are 'earthly, sensual, devilish.' False wisdom. Envy and selfishness breed confusion.

Who is wise? Let him show it by good conduct; but bitter envying and strife are 'earthly, sensual, devilish.' False wisdom. Envy and selfishness breed confusion.
'The wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle'; the fruit of righteousness is 'sown in peace.' Heavenly wisdom. True wisdom is pure and peace-loving.
